statusLine: European fuel stocks are under pressure with 15 of 27 countries below mandatory minimum levels amid elevated oil prices and ongoing Middle East supply concerns.
fullAnalysis:
European fuel security faces significant strain as more than half of EU member states struggle to maintain adequate reserves. A total of 15 of 27 countries have fallen below the mandatory 90-day minimum for at least one fuel type, with Romania, Poland, and Spain experiencing critical shortages across all three fuel categories. Romania presents the most severe situation with petrol reserves at just 43.6 days and diesel at a critically low 20.2 days.

The situation varies considerably by fuel type across the bloc. Jet fuel shows the most widespread vulnerability, with 19 of 27 countries below the 90-day threshold, reflecting aviation sector demand pressures and complex supply chains. Diesel reserves are also concerning, with 14 countries falling short of requirements. Petrol stocks fare relatively better, though 11 countries remain below minimums. Major economies including Germany, France, and Italy all show critical status for multiple fuel types.

Price pressures compound the supply challenges, with Brent crude at $94.79 per barrel representing a 27% increase since the current Middle East conflict began. European consumers face elevated pump prices averaging €1.784 for petrol and €2.021 for diesel, with notable regional variations. The Netherlands leads with petrol at €2.316 per liter, while Malta offers the cheapest at €1.34. These elevated prices, combined with strategic reserve releases due to Middle East supply disruptions, highlight the ongoing vulnerability of European energy security.
keyPoints: 15 of 27 EU countries have fallen below the mandatory 90-day minimum stock requirement for at least one fuel type
Romania faces the most severe shortage with diesel reserves at just 20.2 days, well below the 90-day requirement
Jet fuel shows the broadest vulnerability with 19 of 27 countries below minimum thresholds, averaging 91.1 days EU-wide
Major economies including Germany, France, and Italy all exhibit critical status across multiple fuel categories
Brent crude at $94.79/barrel reflects a 27% increase since the Middle East conflict began, driving elevated consumer fuel prices across Europe
